summary: Reuters reported Intuit would cut 1.7% of global jobs to streamline operations. The tracker counts this as a separate 2026 Intuit event from the company's 2024 AI-focused reorganization, with the headcount estimate derived from Intuit's reported 18,200 employees at the end of fiscal 2025.
evidenceQuote: Intuit to cut 1.7% of global jobs to streamline operations, memo shows.
notes: Reuters verified the new 2026 reduction and described the rationale as streamlining operations. Because the available layoff-specific source language does not directly say AI replaced the affected workers, this is classified conservatively as ai_adjacent_restructuring rather than explicit_ai_cited. The approximate 310-person count is derived from 1.7% of Intuit's 18,200 fiscal-2025 employees.

sources.name: Reuters
sources.quote: Intuit to cut 1.7% of global jobs to streamline operations, memo shows.

sources.name: Intuit 2025 Form 10-K
sources.quote: As of July 31, 2025, we had approximately 18,200 employees in seven countries.

sources.name: Intuit
sources.quote: AI-Native ERP, Intuit Enterprise Suite, is a command center for modern finance teams.
